title: Edge middleware — tenant resolution + CSRF gate + locale + OG rewrite
problem: Every Functions request needs four cross-cutting things done before any handler runs: (1) resolve which tenant this Host serves, (2) gate state-changing /api/* requests against CSRF, (3) on the bare '/' request redirect to the visitor's preferred language, (4) rewrite OG tags in HTML responses so social-share scrapers (which don't run JS) see the right card per-page. Doing any of these per-handler creates four places to forget; centralizing them in _middleware.ts means a new handler inherits the four guards for free. The middleware is the single piece of code every request executes through.
analysis: Single onRequest function called by Cloudflare Pages for every /api/* and SSR path; static assets bypass it. Steps in order: (1) resolveTenant(Host) → ctx.data.tenant — on a 'no such table' error (migration 0002 not yet applied), the request flows through tenant-less so legacy handlers don't break (admin handlers that strictly need a tenant call requireTenant and 500 cleanly); on unknown Host returns a terse 404; on tenant.status='frozen' returns 503 with a 'paused' message. (2) For state-changing /api/* requests (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE), check verifyCsrf unless the path is in CSRF_EXEMPT_PATHS (logout, request-link, admin/digest, payments/webhook, vouches, intake/transcribe). On gate failure, 403 'CSRF mismatch'. (3) For bare '/' GET: read mp_lang cookie OR Accept-Language; if EN preferred, 302 to /en. (4) For HTML responses, use HTMLRewriter to swap OG image, append per-page hreflang link, and rewrite og:url to the absolute canonical. Sentry exception capture wraps everything; uncaught exceptions are reported then rethrown (observe-and-propagate pattern, NOT swallow).

decisions.decision: CSRF gate is centralized here, not per-handler
decisions.rationale: Per-handler CSRF means every new POST/PATCH/DELETE handler must remember to call requireCsrf() — a forgotten one is a silent vulnerability. Centralizing means the default for any new state-changing /api/* path is 'CSRF-protected'; opting out requires editing CSRF_EXEMPT_PATHS, which is reviewable. Every exempt entry carries an inline comment justifying why.
decisions.recommendation: When adding a new POST endpoint, the default is 'no exemption needed' (it inherits the gate). Only add to CSRF_EXEMPT_PATHS if you have a real justification (no cookie context — anon submission OR third-party webhook with its own auth). Document the justification inline.

decisions.decision: Graceful pre-migration tenant fallback ('no such table' → flow through tenantless)
decisions.rationale: Fresh deploys go through a window where the tenant_domains table exists in the migrations directory but hasn't been applied to that environment yet. A hard 'tenant lookup failed' would brick every endpoint until the migration ran. The fallback keeps legacy handlers working; handlers that strictly need a tenant call requireTenant explicitly and 500 cleanly. Once 0002 is universally applied this code path becomes dead but harmless.
decisions.recommendation: Don't remove the 'no such table' branch even if every prod env is past 0002. It's the safety net for any future migration that introduces a new lookup table before handlers expect it.

decisions.decision: OG rewrite via HTMLRewriter, not server-side SSR
decisions.rationale: Pages Functions don't render the SPA — Vite ships static HTML + JS, and the JS is what builds the live DOM. Social scrapers (Facebook, Twitter, Slack, LinkedIn) don't run JS; they grab the static HTML's <meta> tags and quit. SSR would solve this but adds a render layer to maintain. HTMLRewriter streams over the static HTML at request time and patches only the OG-relevant tags — cheap, no SSR, scrapers see the right card per-page. The same rewriter handles hreflang injection.
decisions.recommendation: If a future need requires SSR (e.g. dynamic content above the fold), evaluate whether HTMLRewriter can do it before reaching for a render pipeline. Most 'I need server-side X' for marketing-stack content can be done with edge string substitution at lower complexity.

successCriteria:
- Every Functions request gets ctx.data.tenant attached (or null in the documented fallback case)
- State-changing /api/* paths require CSRF unless explicitly exempt
- Bare '/' GETs respect mp_lang cookie + Accept-Language and 302 to /en when EN-preferred
- Social scrapers see the right OG card per-page (FR vs. EN image, dynamic share card for /share/:id)
- A 'no such table' tenant lookup degrades gracefully, not fatally
- Frozen tenants get a 503 (not a generic error)
- Sentry captures uncaught exceptions then rethrows; nothing is silently swallowed
implementation: functions/_middleware.ts — single onRequest function exported per Pages convention. Helpers: resolveTenant(DB, host) and requireTenant(ctx) in functions/_lib/tenant.ts; verifyCsrf / requireCsrf / currentEmail in functions/_lib/auth.ts; isAdmin in functions/_lib/env.ts. CSRF_EXEMPT_PATHS is a ReadonlySet<string>; each entry carries a justifying inline comment. The locale redirect runs before the CSRF check for GETs (no body to protect). HTMLRewriter is invoked only when the upstream response's content-type includes 'text/html'. Sentry helper captureWorkerException catches + reports + rethrows.
userGuide: Add a new handler under functions/api/ and it inherits: tenant resolution, CSRF for state-changing methods, and OG/hreflang injection (if applicable). To opt out of CSRF: add the path to CSRF_EXEMPT_PATHS with an inline justifying comment. To check the tenant inside a handler: ctx.data.tenant (typed as Tenant | null per the fallback). For tests: see functions/_middleware.test.ts; HTMLRewriter-dependent tests are skipIf'd in environments without it — run under miniflare for full coverage. To debug a CSRF rejection: check that the SPA's mp_csrf cookie is being echoed as X-CSRF-Token in the request (src/lib/api.ts wrap).
knownLimitations: CSRF_EXEMPT_PATHS is hardcoded — there's no dynamic exemption rule (e.g. 'requests from this user-agent'). Static set is intentional; dynamic exemption would be a regression in reviewability
The locale redirect only fires on GET '/' — visitors landing on a non-bare path are assumed to have a language signal in the path; the mp_lang cookie isn't applied to non-bare paths
HTMLRewriter only applies to text/html upstream responses — JSON responses don't get OG injection (correct, but means /api/* paths are skipped explicitly)

Tenant lookup happens on every request; D1 is fast (<10ms typical) but adds a hop. A future cache layer in _lib/tenant.ts could memoize per request — not done today since the cost is unmeasurably small
Sentry reports + rethrows uncaught exceptions; if the SENTRY_DSN secret is unset, captureWorkerException no-ops silently (graceful-degrade)

statusHistory.reason: Middleware shipped with tenant resolution; CSRF gate followed 2026-05-12, OG rewrite 2026-05-15.
Code-first doc co-located. All four guards (tenant, CSRF, locale, OG) have been stable for the past week.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Initial author — P2 critical-API sweep, co-located doc for the edge middleware.
